twitter_thread: 87% of mechanical keyboards use Cherry, Kailh, Outemu, Razer, or Gateron switches. Time to break the mold.
Epomaker's RT98 uses Kailh Box switches, with a 3.5mm travel distance and 45g actuation force.
Key switch lifespan: Cherry MX lasts 50 million presses, Kailh 80 million, and Outemu 70 million.
Per-key customizable backlighting is a must: 16.8 million color options are now the standard.
75% keyboards save 23% of desk space compared to full-size. Is the sacrifice worth it?
Built-in wrist rests increase ergonomy issues by 30%. Separate rests are safer.
What's your go-to mechanical keyboard switch type? #mechkeyboards #gears
pack.linkedin: With 87% of mechanical keyboards using one of five common switch types, exploring alternatives like Kailh Box switches in Epomaker's RT98 can be beneficial. A 75% keyboard layout saves 23% of desk space and per-key customizable backlighting with 16.8 million color options is now standard. What's your experience with mechanical keyboards and preferred switch type or feature set?
